latest.title: Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
latest.content:
25 Mar 2026 07:00
RNS Number : 9578X
IG Design Group PLC
25 March 2026
25 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Completion of surplus UK warehouse sale
IG Design Group plc, a leading designer, innovator and manufacturer across various celebration and creative categories, announces that its subsidiary, IG Design Group UK Limited, has completed the sale of a surplus warehouse at Hirwaun Industrial Estate, Aberdare to the Welsh Government for c.£3.1 million. As previously indicated in the February 2026 trading update, this disposal forms part of the Group's efforts to strengthen its balance sheet. The carrying value of the site was £0.1 million and the sale will realise a profit of £2.9 million (after £0.1m of selling costs and taxes).
​For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison

latest.title: Replacement: Capital Reduction Update
latest.content:
4 Mar 2026 13:20
RNS Number : 3511V
IG Design Group PLC
04 March 2026
The following amendment has been made to the Capital Reduction Update announcement released on 4 March 2026 at 12:15 under RNS No 3439V ("the Announcement").
The Announcement should have been dated 4 March 2026. This amendment has been made below.
All other details remain unchanged.
4 March 2026
IG Design Group plc
(the "Company", the "Group" or "Design Group")
Capital Reduction Update
The board of directors of IG Design Group plc is pleased to announce that the reduction of capital (the "
Capital Reduction
") required to effect the cancellation of the Capital Reduction Shares issued by the Company prior to the Court Hearing together with share premium and the capital redemption reserve was approved on 3 March 2026 by the High Court of Justice of England and Wales (the "
Court
").
The Court order confirming the Capital Reduction and a statement of capital approved by the Court in connection therewith will shortly be sent to the Registrar of Companies. The Capital Reduction will become effective upon the registration of the Court order and associated statement of capital by the Registrar of Companies. Completion of the Capital Reduction will not affect the rights attaching to the Ordinary Shares and will not result in any change to the number of Ordinary Shares in issue (or their nominal value).
The Capital Reduction will allow the Company to potentially make future shareholder distributions.
Full details of the Capital Reduction are set out in the notice of extraordinary general meeting which was sent to shareholders of the Company on 15 January 2026 (the "
EGM Circular
"). The EGM Circular is also available for inspection on the Company website,
Reports and Presentations - Design Group
. Unless otherwise stated, defined terms in this announcement are the same as those set out in the EGM Circular. As set out in the EGM Circular, provisional dates were previously obtained by the Company for the required Court Hearings, which were subsequently changed at the discretion of the Company.
For further information, please contact:
IG Design Group plc
Tel: +44 (0)1525 887310
Stewart Gilliland, Interim Executive Chair
Rohan Cummings, Chief Financial Officer
Canaccord Genuity Limited (Nomad and Broker)
Tel: +44 (0)20 7523 8000
Bobbie Hilliam
Elizabeth Halley-Stott
Alma Strategic Communications
Tel: +44 (0)20 3405 0205
Rebecca Sanders-Hewett
designgroup@almastrategic.com
Sam Modlin
Will Merison
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: IG Design Group makes and sells decorative items for celebrations, such as party supplies, giftware and seasonal decorations. They design, manufacture and distribute these products to retailers, wholesalers and event‑organisers.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The party‑goods market is huge and growing, especially with the rise of online retail and DIY event planning. IG Design Group has a long‑standing brand in the UK and a catalogue that spans from small gifts to large festive displays, giving it a foothold in both consumer and corporate sectors.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently sold a surplus warehouse and announced a capital‑reduction plan, signalling a shift to a leaner balance sheet. It is still operating at a modest scale and has a mix of cash and short‑term liabilities, but the latest announcements suggest it is cutting costs and attracting new institutional interest.

rns_narrative: The company has been divesting non‑core assets – a UK warehouse was sold to the Welsh government – and is simultaneously shrinking its share capital through a capital‑reduction plan. These moves are aimed at improving liquidity and reducing dilution for existing shareholders.

Corporate governance is in the spotlight: an extraordinary general meeting has been held, new holdings are being disclosed and an institutional investor has been mentioned in recent posts. These signals hint at a potential stabilisation of ownership and a more disciplined capital structure.
Overall, the narrative is one of a company trimming excess, tightening its balance sheet and positioning itself for a more focused, potentially higher‑margin business model.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism with sporadic bullish chatter
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: A handful of traders are pointing to the warehouse sale and the capital‑reduction as evidence of a clean‑up that could lift the stock. The mention of an institutional investor adds a layer of credibility to a potential upside.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: A few voices warn that the company’s revenue base is thin and that the capital‑reduction might simply be a last‑ditch effort to keep the shares alive. Some fear that further dilution or a lack of new orders could push the price lower again.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has stayed flat, with most posts being neutral or mildly bullish. There is no active debate, indicating a quiet market that could be primed for a surprise move.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends for IGR.L is dead, giving the setup a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the warehouse sale and the finalisation of the capital‑reduction plan – both are already announced and should materialise in the coming weeks.
Any announcement of new institutional ownership or a new product launch that could create demand for the company’s decorative items.
A spike in trading volume that exceeds the 30‑day average, signalling renewed interest from traders or investors.
expect_next.watch_for: Confirm the price climbs above the last swing low and that volume is at least twice the 30‑day average. A break of the 20‑day moving average would also support a mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock is still in the early stages of a recovery cycle – the APEX score suggests a tight compression, implying that a breakout could be imminent. The deep crash indicates capitulation is likely complete, so the next move is a potential mean‑reversion.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: IG Design Group’s stock is down over 80% from its peak, which is a classic deep‑crash scenario that historically delivers the biggest rebounds. The capital‑reduction cuts dilution, and the sale of the warehouse improves liquidity. If the price rebounds to roughly the halfway point of its peak, that would be a 150‑200% gain from the current level.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company still has a thin revenue stream and could face further dilution if it needs to raise more capital. A failure to secure new orders or institutional interest could push the price lower still, and the company could become a target for a takeover or delisting if fundamentals remain weak.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 150‑200% from current price. Potential downside: 100% to zero. With a tight stop 20% below entry, the risk‑reward ratio is roughly 2:1, favouring a contrarian long.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when the price breaks above the most recent swing low with volume at least twice the 30‑day average. Size the position for 5% of your discretionary capital, set a stop 20% below entry, and trail the stop by 10% as the price climbs.
playbook.if_watching: Hold a small position until the price touches the 30%‑of‑peak level and volume spikes, then consider a larger entry. Watch for a formal announcement of the capital‑reduction completion or a new institutional investor filing.
playbook.if_passing: If the company files for bankruptcy, announces a delisting, or the share price falls below 50% of the recent low with no signs of recovery, exit immediately.

ai_insights.bottom_line: IG Design Group sits on a deep crash that, coupled with an asset sale and a capital‑reduction plan, could trigger a strong mean‑reversion; the upside is significant while the downside can be limited with tight stops.

Historical data shows 3 rallies averaging 201% upside. Current position: -39.4%. Your edge comes from waiting for AI Engine confirmation instead of blind entry.
